source.name: St. Augustine Municipal Marina / Waterway Guide

standard.rate_basis: daily transient dockage available; official rate sheet uses vessel size tiers
standard.notes: Hourly dockage is available at $5 per hour up to 4 hours when space is available; dinghy dockage permits are $15/day.

cautions.text: Current in the downtown waterfront area can be meaningful, especially around the Bridge of Lions.

expert_notes.plain: This is the downtown convenience play, not the cheapest play. Strong overall stop if you want services, dinghy access, and immediate access to town.

source.name: City of St. Augustine / Waterway Guide

standard.notes: Monthly transient mooring rate listed as $620.

cautions.text: Expect current and harbor movement in the downtown mooring area.

expert_notes.plain: Good downtown mooring choice if you want to be in the middle of St. Augustine without paying full-slip rates.

source.name: City of St. Augustine / Waterway Guide

standard.notes: Monthly transient mooring rate listed as $400.

cautions.text: Salt Run has limited availability, minimum shoreside amenities, and no water tender service.
The water in Salt Run tends to be much shallower, so draft deserves attention.

expert_notes.plain: This is the quieter and more budget-friendly St. Augustine mooring play, but it is much less convenient ashore.

source.name: Camachee Cove Yacht Harbor / Waterway Guide

standard.notes: Transient slips available; verify current rates directly.

cautions.text: Current transient pricing should be confirmed directly.

expert_notes.plain: This is the protected, calmer St. Augustine marina option rather than the budget option.

standard.notes: Transient slips available; verify current rates directly.

cautions.text: Current transient pricing should be confirmed directly.

expert_notes.plain: Good provisioning-oriented stop with a more practical, less touristy feel than downtown.

standard.notes: 198 slips and 69 moorings; verify current transient and mooring rates directly.

cautions.text: Current transient and mooring pricing should be confirmed directly.

expert_notes.plain: This is the most complete Stuart marina/anchorage package in the current core file, with both slip and mooring options plus strong services.

standard.notes: Transient slips available; verify current rates directly.

cautions.text: Current transient pricing should be confirmed directly.

expert_notes.plain: A stronger Stuart marina option for protection and services than for budget.

standard.notes: Weeks 1-2 at $2.00/ft/day; weeks 3-16 at $1.25/ft/day; 5-11 months at $23.75/ft.

cautions.text: Official dockage rates are published by the city but should still be confirmed before arrival.

expert_notes.plain: A strong municipal-marina style stop with real services, published rates, and better budget transparency than many private marinas.

source.name: City of Vero Beach / Waterway Guide

standard.rate_basis: per ft per month transient slip
standard.notes: Transient daily mooring rate is $23.36. Waterway Guide lists 10 transient slips.

cautions.text: Vero’s official published rate structure uses monthly-equivalent formatting in places; confirm the exact transient slip quote directly.

expert_notes.plain: This is a strong managed-city-marina option with both mooring and transient capability, and better published rate transparency than many private marinas.

standard.notes: Please call for pricing; marina leases by slip size rather than vessel size. Current transient rates should be confirmed directly.

cautions.text: This is not a good choice for deeper-draft boats; reported approach and dockside depth are only 5 feet.

expert_notes.plain: Useful small-slip Riviera Beach option if your draft fits it. Better for modest-draft boats than for heavier cruising sailboats.

geo.segment: Palm Beach Shores / Lake Worth Inlet

standard.notes: Current transient pricing should be confirmed directly.

cautions.text: Current transient pricing should be confirmed directly.

expert_notes.plain: A legitimate Lake Worth inlet-area transient stop with easy boater contact information and on-site fuel, but not the cheapest play.

source.name: Waterway Guide / Marina Village Palm Beach

standard.rate_basis: see marina website for current transient rates
standard.notes: Dockage available for long-term, monthly, or transient use; call ahead for current pricing.

cautions.text: Current can be an issue, especially on the outer docks, so docking plans should account for tide and current.
Reported dockside depth is only 7 feet, so deeper-draft boats should confirm current conditions carefully.

expert_notes.plain: A real Riviera Beach transient option with decent services and fuel, but current on the outer docks is the operational issue to take seriously.

standard.notes: Current transient rates should be confirmed directly.

cautions.text: This is a premium deep-water marina and service yard rather than a budget stop.

expert_notes.plain: A deep-water, high-capability Palm Beach area stop that makes sense when draft, boat size, or service yard access matter more than budget.

source.name: Waterway Guide / Town of Palm Beach Marina

standard.rate_basis: contact facility or official dockage page for current pricing
standard.notes: Call ahead to confirm current pricing and availability.

cautions.text: This is a premier Palm Beach marina, so pricing should be expected to reflect that.

expert_notes.plain: A strong Palm Beach choice when location and quality matter more than cost. Excellent walkability and serious transient capability.

standard.rate_basis: contact marina for current transient pricing
standard.notes: Transient overnight and transient monthly slips are available; current pricing should be confirmed directly.

cautions.text: Current transient rates should be confirmed directly.

expert_notes.plain: A very useful West Palm transient marina with real contactability, fuel, and downtown access. Strong choice when you want a proper marina rather than the public docks.

source.name: Fernandina Harbor Marina / City of Fernandina Beach

standard.rate_basis: published by vessel-length tiers; call marina for current quote and availability
standard.notes: Transient dockage is published by length tier. Under 70 ft: $2.75-$3.25/ft/day, $16.10-$17.50/ft/week, $34.50/ft/month. 70-99 ft: $3.00-$3.50/ft/day, $17.15-$18.20/ft/week, $38.40/ft/month. 100-149 ft: $3.50-$4.00/ft/day, $19.25-$20.00/ft/week, $42.00/ft/month. 150-199 ft: $3.75-$4.25/ft/day, $21.00-$22.50/ft/week, $43.50/ft/month. 200-250 ft: $4.00-$4.50/ft/day, $22.75-$24.15/ft/week, $43.50/ft/month. Special events and holidays may be higher. Transient stays limited to 6 months. Long-term dockage: non-commercial $25/ft/month; commercial $27.50/ft/month. Dock N Dine per 3 hours: up to 25 ft $16.05; 25-35 ft $32.10; over 36 ft $42.80. Electric: daily 30A/50A/100A = $6/$12/$25; weekly $30/$72/$125; monthly $60/$125/$350.

cautions.text: This is a busy downtown waterfront marina, so movement and activity can be higher than at more secluded stops.
Special events and holidays can carry higher rates than the standard published schedule.

expert_notes.plain: A top Fernandina stop if downtown access matters. Strong contactability, fuel, pumpout, and immediate walkability make it the practical in-town choice. The marina also has a first-come, first-served mooring field with amenity access.

source.name: Fernandina Harbor Marina official rates page

standard.notes: First come, first served mooring field. Weekly mooring rate is $192.60 tax included. Monthly mooring rate is $636.64 tax included.

special_offers.label: Dinghy dock under 12 ft
special_offers.notes: Tax included; vessels less than 12 feet only

special_offers.label: Dinghy dock under 12 ft
special_offers.notes: Tax included; vessels less than 12 feet only

cautions.text: The mooring field is first come, first served.
Dinghy dock pricing applies only to vessels less than 12 feet.

expert_notes.plain: The Fernandina mooring field is first come, first served. Amenity access includes the dinghy dock, showers, laundry, free Wi-Fi, and the boater's lounge.

source.name: Windward at Amelia Island Marina / CruisersNet / Dockwa

standard.notes: Approach is shallow enough that tide and current conditions should be checked before committing.

cautions.text: This is not a good blind-arrival stop for deeper boats. Reported approach depth is shallow enough that current local conditions matter.

expert_notes.plain: A quieter Amelia-area stop than downtown Fernandina, but the approach depth is the real filter. Better for shallower boats that want protection more than town access.

source.name: Tiger Point Marina / Waterway Guide / Official Marina Site

standard.notes: Transient space is more limited than at some larger nearby stops, so call ahead.

cautions.text: Transient space can be limited, so this is best treated as a call-ahead stop.

expert_notes.plain: A practical Amelia River stop when you want marina services and a quieter setting than downtown Fernandina.
